title: M/S Icici Bank Ltd, Rep By Pramodh G vs Roseanne Joseph on 17 August, 2024
body: M/S Icici Bank Ltd, Rep By Pramodh G vs Roseanne Joseph on 17 August, 2024 KABC0A0004832022 C.R.P.67 Govt. of Karnataka Form No.9 (Civil) Title Sheet for Judgments in Suits (R.P.91) TITLE SHEET FOR JUDGMENTS IN SUITS IN THE COURT OF THE XXVIII ADDITIONAL CITY CIVIL AND SESSIONS JUDGE (CCH-29) MAYOHALL, BENGALURU Dated this the 17th day of August, 2024. PRESENT: Sri BALAPPA APPANNA JARAGU B.Sc., LL.M., XXVIII Additional City Civil and Sessions Judge, Bengaluru. ORIGINAL SUIT No.25221/2022 PLAINTIFF : M/s. ICICI Bank Limited, Having its registered off at Mytree Centre, No.4, 101 Hosur Road, Bommanahalli, Bangalore - 560 068. Represented by its Debt Collection Manager, Sri Pramod G., S/o. Gurumallppa B. (By Smt. Rjani J., Advocate) -VERSUS- DEFENDANT : Mrs. Roseanne Joseph, Aged about 48 years, D/o. Ebinizer Joseph, Residing at Flat No.107, IJ Acacia Apartments, Block 1, Cont'd.. 2 O.S.No.25221/2022 Seegehalli, Virgonagar Post, Bangalore - 560 049. (Ex-parte) --------------------------------------------------------------------- Date of Institution of the Suit : 03-02-2022 Nature of the Suit (Suit on : Money Suit pronote, Suit for declaration and possession, Suit for injunction etc,) Date of the commencement : 30-07-2024 of recording of the evidence Date on which the Judgment : 17-08-2024 was pronounced --------------------------------------------------------------------- Year/s Month/s Day/s ---------------------------------- Total duration : 02years, 06months, 14days. --------------------------------------------------------------------- (BALAPPA APPANNA JARAGU) XXVIII Additional City Civil and Sessions Judge, Mayohall, Bengaluru. JUDGMENT This is a suit instituted by the plaintiff Bank against the defendant for recovery of suit claim amount of Rs.9,92,502/- along with future interest at the rate of 11.35% per annum from date of suit, till realization and for such other reliefs. 3 O.S.No.25221/2022 2. The case of the plaintiff in brief is as under: That, defendant has approached plaintiff bank seeking personal loan of Rs.10,00,000/-. Considering request of defendant, on 12.09.2018 plaintiff bank has advanced loan of Rs.10,00,000/- to the defendant. The defendant has agreed to repay loan amount in 60 Equated Monthly installments of Rs.21,904/- each commencing from 10.10.2018. The defendant has also agreed to repay loan amount along with interest at rate of 11.35% per annum. The defendant has executed all necessary documents to the plaintiff bank for having availed loan. Initially defendant was prompt in repayment of installments. Subsequently, the defendant committed default to pay loan amount. Even after adjusting said amount paid by defendant to his loan, he is still due in a sum of Rs.9,92,502/-. On these grounds, the plaintiff bank has filed present suit against defendant for relief as prayed in the plaint. 3. Though suit summons issued by the court served on the defendant, she has not tendered her appearance before the court and contested the suit, consequently she was placed ex-parte. 4 O.S.No.25221/2022 4. On the basis of plaint averments and other documents produced before the court, the following points arises for my consideration:- POINTS 1. Whether plaintiff bank proves that, the defendant has availed loan of Rs.10,00,000/- from plaintiff bank for personal needs? 2. Whether plaintiff bank further proves that, after availment of loan, the defendant become defaulter or irregular in payment of loan amount and she is in due of Rs. 9,92,502/- to the plaintiff bank? 3. Whether plaintiff bank further proves that, even after repeated requests and demand, the defendant is not ready to pay remaining loan amount along with agreed interest? 4. Whether the plaintiff is entitled for the relief as sought for? 5. What Order or Decree?? 5 O.S.No.25221/2022 5. The Collection Manager of plaintiff Bank got examined himself as P.W.1 and got marked documents as per Exs.P.1 to Ex.P.8 and closed its side. 6. I have heard the arguments of learned counsel for plaintiff and I have perused the case records. 7. After close scrutiny of oral and documentary evidence and after hearing the arguments, my findings to the above points are as follows:- POINT No.1 - In the affirmative; POINT No.2 - In the affirmative; POINT No.3 - In the affirmative; POINT No.4 - Partly in the affirmative; POINT No.5 - As per final order, for the following - REASONS 8. POINT NOS.1 TO 4 :- As these points are inter related to each other, involve common appreciation of facts and evidence on record, findings on one point are bearing on other point, as such in order avoid repetition of facts and for convenience sake, these points taken together for common discussion. 6 O.S.No.25221/2022 9. The plaintiff Bank in order to prove that, defendant has availed personal loan from plaintiff bank has produced loan application dated 12.09.2018 as per Ex.P.3. Preliminary credit facility application Form dated 11.09.2018 has been produced as per Ex.P.2. Fact statement information has been produced as per Ex.P.4. The Manager of Bank who examined as P.W.1 in his evidence before the court has specifically stated, the plaintiff Bank has sanctioned loan of Rs.10,00,000/- to the defendant on 12.09.2018. The defendant has executed necessary documents in favour of plaintiff Bank for having availed loan. 10. It is further case of plaintiff Bank and same is deposed by P.W.1 that, after availing loan, defendant has initially paid some amount towards loan and thereafter he become defaulter and thereby failed to repay installments with interest thereon. The plaintiff Bank has also sent demand notices to the defendant. In order to show the plaintiff Bank has issued notice to the defendant, copy of the notice produced as per Exs.P.5 and P.6. 7 O.S.No.25221/2022 11. The plaintiff Bank has pleaded and it is deposed by P.W.1 in his examination chief that, as on date of filing suit, the defendant has to pay an amount of Rs. 9,92,502/- to the plaintiff Bank. In order to show that, defendant has to pay said mentioned amount to the plaintiff Bank, the statements of account has been produced as per Ex.P.7. In Ex.P.7 it is mentioned as on date of filing suit, the defendant has to pay an amount of Rs.9,92,502/- to the plaintiff Bank. 12. On combined appreciation of oral evidence of PW.1 and documentary evidence as per Exs.P.1 to Ex.P.7 it can be very well gathered, the defendant has availed loan of Rs. 10,00,000/- from plaintiff bank agreeing to repay loan with interest at rate of banking interest 11.35% per annum. It is also proved by plaintiff bank that, even after issuance of notice, the defendant has not repaid loan amount with interest as agreed by him. 13. It is to be noted here, even after service of suit summons, the defendant has not appeared before the court through his counsel and contested the case. 8 O.S.No.25221/2022 Inference can be drawn that, the defendant is admitting oral evidence and documentary evidence produced before the court by plaintiff Bank. Further inference can be drawn that, the defendant has no valid and legal defense against claim of plaintiff Bank. In view of non- contesting of suit by defendant even after service of suit summons, oral and documentary evidence adduced by plaintiff Bank in support of suit claim remained unchallenged and uncontroverted. The plaintiff Bank with support of documentary evidence and oral evidence of P.W.1 has sufficiently proved, the defendant is under obligation to pay suit claim amount to the plaintiff Bank with reasonable interest. 14. The plaintiff Bank has requested the court to pass decree directing the defendant to pay interest @ 11.35% P.A. on suit claim from date of suit, till its actual realization. As per Sec. 34 of CPC the court may order interest at such rate as deems reasonable to be paid on principal from date of suit to the date of decree and from date of decree to date of payment. Looking into purpose for which the defendant has availed loan 9 O.S.No.25221/2022 from plaintiff Bank, the court can award interest at such rate not exceeding 6% per annum. 15. As already discussed above, the defendant has availed loan for personal use. Hence, taking into consideration of purpose for which the defendant has availed loan, interest on suit claim from date of suit till its actual realization would be 6% per annum.. Hence, I answer Point No.1 to 3 in the Affirmative and Point No.4 Partly in the Affirmative. 16. Point No.5:- In view of the above said findings on Points No. 1 to 3 In the Affirmative and Point No.4 Partly in the Affirmative, I proceed to pass the following: ORDER The suit of the plaintiff bank is hereby partly decreed with costs. The plaintiff bank is entitled to recover suit claim of Rs.9,92,502/- from defendant with interest at rate of 6% per annum from date of suit, till its actual realization. 10 O.S.No.25221/2022 The defendant is liable to pay amount to the plaintiff bank as mentioned above within 2 months from date of decree. In case defendant fails to pay amount to the plaintiff bank as mentioned above within time fixed, the plaintiff bank is at liberty to recover the same by following due procedure known to law. Draw decree accordingly. (Dictated to the Stenographer directly on computer, typed & computerized by her, corrected and signed by me and then pronounced in the open Court on this the 17th day of August, 2024). (BALAPPA APPANNA JARAGU) XXVIII Additional City Civil and Sessions Judge, Mayohall, Bengaluru. ANNEXURE 1. LIST OF WITNESSES EXAMINED FOR THE PLAINTIFF:- Examined on: P.W.1 : Pramodh Gurumallappa 30-07-2024 2. LIST OF DOCUMENTS MARKED FOR THE PLAINTIFF:- Ex.P.1 : Notarized GPA. Ex.P.2 : Loan application. 11 O.S.No.25221/2022 Ex.P.3 : Credit facility application form. Ex.P.4 : Key fact statement and most important information. Ex.P.5 : Loan recall notice. Ex.P.6 : Pre-payment Statement. Ex.P.7 : Statement of accounts. Ex.P.8 : Certificate under Sec.2A(B) and 2A(c) of the bankers book Evidence Act, 1891 . 3.LIST OF WITNESSES EXAMINED FOR THE DEFENDANT:- -None- 4.LIST OF DOCUMENTS MARKED FOR THE DEFENDANT:- NIL (BALAPPA APPANNA JARAGU) XXVIII Additional City Civil and Sessions Judge, Mayohall, Bengaluru.
